content.full_text:
June 26, 1978


Mr. Norton T. Hildreth, Treasurer
"Yes on 13" Committee
1674 Willow Creek Drive
San Jose, California 95124

Dear Mr. Hildreth:

     This is in response to your June 10, 1978, letter concerning
your reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act. You
state in your letter that you have been treasurer of the "Yes on
13 Committee, ("Committee") ID #781168, and as such responsible
for keeping records of contributions received and expenditures
made. However, without your knowledge, a separate bank account
was established into which contributions were deposited and from
which expenditures were made. It is not clear from your letter
whether the second bank account was opened in the name of the
Committee. I will assume for purposes of this letter that it was.

     At some point in time, the records for this second account
were turned over to you. Since these records are incomplete as
to the required information on contributions and expenditures,
you have asked how you as treasurer can fulfill your responsibil-
ities for the post-election campaign statement due no later than
July 31, 1978.

     Government Code Section 84100 describes the obligations of
a treasurer and the treasurer's agents. The relevant subsections
are:

     (b)  No expenditure shall be made by or on behalf
          of a committee without the authorization of
          the treasurer or that of his designated agents.

     (c)  All contributions received by a person acting
          as an agent of a candidate shall be reported
          promptly by such person to the candidate or
          any of his designated agents. All contributions
          received by a person acting as an agent of
          a committee shall be reported promptly by the
          recipient to the committee's treasurer or any
          of his designated agents. "Promptly" as used
          in this section means not later than five days

Mr. Norton T. Hildreth                -2-                June 26, 1978


before the closing date of any campaign statement
required to be filed by the treasurer, and
immediately if the contribution was received
less than five days before the closing date. All
contributions shall be segregated from and may not
be commingled with any personal funds of the
recipient or any other person.

Keeping those subsections in mind and the requirement under
Government Code Section 81004 that the treasurer must verify a
campaign statement stating that he used reasonable diligence and
that the campaign statement is true and complete to the best of
his knowledge, I hope the following comments are helpful.

If the individuals who opened the second bank account are
your agents, they are required, under Government Code Section
84100(c), to report to you contributions received by them. They
are required to provide you, as treasurer, with complete information "promptly", that is, not later than five days before the
closing date of the post-election campaign statement of June 25,
1978. You should include on the Committee's campaign statement
the information you are provided concerning the contributions
and expenditures of the second bank account. To the extent the
information is not complete, you could attach an explanation to
your campaign statement. An amendment should be filed when additional information is later obtained.

If the individuals are not your agents, they may have qualified as a separate recipient committee under Government Code
Section 82013(a) with an obligation to register with the
Secretary of State's Office and file a Statement of Organization
and campaign statements.

If the Committee has surplus funds after its activity has
ceased, those funds may be disposed of in accordance with the
Committee's desires. The payment of the surplus funds will be
reportable as an expenditure by the Committee on a campaign
statement.

If the Committee decides, after filing the post-election
campaign statement, that it wishes to terminate, a Statement of
Termination could be filed with the appropriate filing officer.
However, a Statement of Termination can only be filed if the
Committee can meet the conditions outlined in the enclosed informational sheet and the Fair Political Practices Commission's
regulation on termination of committees, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18404. For your possible use, I am enclosing a copy of Form 415.

Mr. Norton T. Hildreth                -3-                June 26, 1978


     Regina Siciliano-Kutchins of our staff has spoken with
Mr. Robert Barrett, Chairman of the Committee, and explained the
above advice to him and indicated that he would receive a copy
of this response.

     If you have any questions, please contact me or Ms. Siciliano-
Kutchins, by calling collect, (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        John C. Greenwood
                                        Technical Assistance Program
                                        Coordinator

JCG

Enclosures

Mr. Norton T. Hildreth, Treasurer
"Yes on 13" Committee
1674 Willow Creek Drive
San Jose, California 95124

Dear Mr. Hildreth:

     This is in response to your June 10, 1978, letter concerning
your reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act. You
state in your letter that you have been treasurer of the "Yes" on
13 Committee,("Committee") ID #781168, and as such responsible
for keeping records of contributions received and expenditures
made.  However, without your knowledge, a separate bank account

Mr. Norton T. Hildreth                    -2-                    June 26, 1978


                    before the closing date of any campaign statement

                    immediately if the contribution was received

     Keeping those subsections in mind and the requirement under
Government Code Section 81004 that the treasurer must verify a
campaign statement stating that he used reasonable diligence and
that the campaign statement is true and complete to the best of
his knowledge, I hope the following comments are helpful.

     If the individuals who opened the second bank account are
your agents, they are required, under Government Code Section
84100(c), to report to you contributions received by them. They
are required to provide you, as treasurer, with complete informa-
tion "promptly", that is, not later than five days before the
closing date of the post-election campaign statement or June 25,
1978. You should include on the Committee's campaign statement
the information you are provided concerning the contributions
and expenditures of the second bank account. To the extent the
information is not complete, you could attach an explanation to
your campaign statement. An amendment should be filed when addi-
tional information is later obtained.

     If the individuals are not your agents, they may have quali-
fied as a separate recipient committee under Government Code
Section 82013(a) with an obligation to register with the
Secretary of State's Office and file a Statement of Organization
and campaign statements.

     If the Committee has surplus funds after its activity has
ceased, those funds may be disposed of in accordance with the
Committee's desires. The payment of the surplus funds will be
reportable as an expenditure by the Committee on a campaign
statement.

     If the Committee decides, after filing the post-election
campaign statement, that it wishes to terminate, a Statement of
Termination could be filed with the appropriate filing officer.
However, a Statement of Termination can only be filed if the
Committee can meet the conditions outlined in the enclosed infor-
mational sheet and the Fair Political Practices Commission's
regulation on termination of committees, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18404. For your possible use, I am enclosing a copy of Form 415.

Mr. Norton T. Hildreth                    -3-                    June 26, 1978


     Regina Siciliano-Kutchins of our staff has spoken with
Mr. Robert Barrett, Chairman of the Committee, and explained the
above advice to him and indicated that he would receive a copy
of this response.

     If you have any questions, please contact me or Ms. Siciliano-
Kutchins, by calling collect, (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        John C. Greenwood
                                        Technical Assistance Program
                                        Coordinator

JCG

Enclosures

cc: Robert Barrett

'YES' on 13 Committee
with 'NO' ON 8
300 Railway Avenue - Campbell, California 95008
(408) 379-4611

June 20, 1978

The Honorable March Fong Eu, Secretary of State
c/o Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

SUBJECT: Accounting Procedure #781168

Dear Madam Secretary:

I am puzzled as to why I have not received
any response from your office. I am awaiting some
response as to what I should do about the second bank
account which was established.

My final report is due soon and I do need
advice.

As stated in the previous letter, copy of
which is attached, we are available to come to Sacramento
and discuss this matter - or will meet with someone from
your office here in San Jose.

Thank you for an early reply.

Respectfully,

Norton T. Hildreth
Norton T. Hildreth, Treasurer
"Yes on 13" Committee
1674 Willow Creek Drive
San Jose, California 95124

lh

Telephone: Home - 408-266-2764
Work - 408-925-6533

● The "YES" on 13 ("NO" on 8) Committee seeks tax relief for home-
owners, renters, mobile home tenants and businesses. Remember a "NO"
on 8 is as important as a "YES" on 13.
Printed by General Printing Co.          885 W. Julian St., San Jose          ⊗ ⊗

June 10, 1978

The Honorable March Fong Eu, Secretary of State
c/o Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814
SUBJECT: Accounting Procedure #781168

Dear Madam Secretary:

My duties as acceptor and dispenser of funds (treasurer) to be
used on behalf of Proposition 13 have been plagued by a problem which
became known to me during the past few weeks which requires a decision
to be made by yourself as the Honorable Secretary of State, or your
Staff, with jurisdiction over the "Fair Political Practices Commission"
relating to elections.

The "Yes on 13" Committee, 300 Railway Avenue, Campbell, Ca.
95008, has an established checking account at the First National Bank
of San Jose for which in-person or by-mail contributions are received,
identified, and receipted by the staff. The daily receipts are then
turned over to the treasurer for deposit in the checking account.

It has developed that a second banking account was established by
a member of the headquarters staff without my knowledge. I do not
know the actual receipts or expenditures, if any, from this account.
Two persons involved volunteered to turn over the account - seemingly
with incomplete itemized receipts and expenditures. I am at a loss
as to the technique for clearing up the situation in compliance with
the law and in preparation for closing the report period of June 30th.

I will be available for further information - either in Sacramento
or here in San Jose - at your discretion. My home telephone number
is 408-266-2764 - my work telephone is 408-925-6533.

Thank you for whatever advice you can give.

Respectfully,

Norton T. Hildreth
Norton T. Hildreth, Treasurer
"Yes on 13" Committee

1674 Willow Creek Drive
San Jose, California 95124

NTH/lh

1674 Willow Creek Drive

sections.conclusion: If the individuals who opened the second bank account are your agents, they are required under Government Code Section 84100(c) to report to you contributions received by them promptly. You should include on the Committee's campaign statement the information you are provided concerning the contributions and expenditures of the second bank account. To the extent the information is not complete, you could attach an explanation to your campaign statement. An amendment should be filed when additional information is later obtained. If the individuals are not your agents, they may have qualified as a separate recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a) with an obligation to register and file statements.
sections.question_synthetic: As treasurer of the "Yes on 13" Committee, how should the requester handle incomplete records from a second bank account established without their knowledge when preparing the post-election campaign statement due July 31, 1978?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If the individuals who opened the second account are the treasurer's agents, they must report all contributions promptly and the treasurer should include the information on the campaign statement with explanations for any gaps, filing amendments as additional information becomes available. If they are not agents, they may be a separate committee required to register independently.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letters (June 10 and June 20, 1978) from Norton T. Hildreth and FPPC's June 26, 1978 response letter. Primary response letter clearly structured with regulatory citations and advice. Some OCR quality issues noted in duplicate pages but main response content is clear and complete.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to the "Yes on 13" Committee treasurer regarding how to handle incomplete records from an unauthorized second bank account when filing the required post-election campaign statement. The FPPC advises that agent status determines reporting obligations and provides guidance on including available information with explanatory notes.
